sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Valencia has had mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, including a heavy 4-0 defeat to Real Madrid. Levante UD, similarly struggling, has 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses in their recent 5 games, conceding multiple goals in most matches. Both teams are in the relegation zone, with Valencia at 17th (10 points) and Levante at 19th (9 points), indicating a desperate need for points.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Valencia has been unbeaten, securing a 4-3 victory in 2021 and a 1-1 draw in 2022. Historically, matches between these two sides tend to be tight, with low-scoring outcomes (less than 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 meetings) but high in physicality, as evidenced by more than 4.5 cards in all recent games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Valencia, Arnaut Danjuma and Hugo Duro are critical in attack, each contributing 3 goals this season. Levante UD will rely on Etta Eyong, who has scored 6 goals and is ranked 3rd in the league for goals, posing a significant threat upfront.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Valencia struggles with defensive solidity, conceding 21 goals this season (18th in the league), while Levante has leaked 23 goals, also near the bottom. Both teams have shown vulnerabilities in maintaining clean sheets, with Levante failing to do so in their last 6 matches. Expect a game with potential scoring opportunities for both sides but likely marred by defensive errors.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Trends
sections.text: Valencia averages 4.92 corners per game, while Levante’s matches often see over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring in 5 of their last 6 games. With the referee, Alejandro Muñiz Ruiz, averaging 5.1 yellow cards per game, and historical head-to-head data showing high card counts, a physical encounter is anticipated.

sections.subtitle: Prediction
sections.text: Considering Sofascore’s probabilities (Valencia 40%, Draw 25%, Levante 35%) and both teams’ poor form, a draw seems plausible. Valencia’s slight edge at home in Mestalla and unbeaten record against Levante in recent years tilt the balance marginally in their favor, but Levante’s attacking threat via Eyong could exploit Valencia’s defensive frailties. A narrow, high-intensity draw is the most likely outcome.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the score remains goalless between Valencia and Levante UD at the Mestalla. Despite the lack of goals, the first half saw both teams creating opportunities, with Valencia holding a slight edge in possession and showing more intent in the attacking third. Levante UD, on the other hand, have been more physical, winning a higher percentage of duels and aerial challenges, which could pose problems for Valencia if they continue this trend.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Valencia's performance
halftimeSections.text: Valencia have dominated the ball for much of the first half, consistently entering the final third and penetrating the penalty area with relative ease. They’ve created a notable chance but failed to convert, which might weigh on their confidence. Their duel success rate is slightly concerning, as they’ve struggled to match Levante UD's physicality. Players like André Almeida and Thierry Correia have shown promise with their contributions in attack and defense, respectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Levante UD's performance
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD have adopted a more conservative approach, focusing on disrupting Valencia’s rhythm and winning possession in key areas. Their frequent entries into the final third suggest they are not entirely defensive, and they’ve shown resilience in duels, particularly in the air. Discipline remains an issue, as they’ve picked up more bookings than their opponents. Players like Jeremy Toljan and Unai Elgezabal have been solid at the back, helping to neutralize Valencia’s attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with Valencia asserting dominance early on, pushing forward with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes. Momentum briefly shifted to Levante UD around the midway point of the half, as they mounted a series of threatening moves into Valencia’s territory. However, Valencia regained control towards the latter stages of the half, finishing strongly with sustained pressure. Levante UD showed flashes of danger late in the half, hinting at their potential to strike if given space.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: In the second half, Valencia will need to capitalize on their possession and territorial advantage to break the deadlock. Converting their chances will be crucial, as missed opportunities could invite pressure from Levante UD. For the visitors, maintaining their physical edge and exploiting any defensive lapses from Valencia could be the key to nicking a goal. Set pieces might play a significant role given Levante UD’s aerial strength and Valencia’s corner count.
